principles.authority_is_not_geography: Spatial coverage describes where an entity operates or is located. It does NOT determine legal authority. Authority is held in the truth-layer Authority records.
principles.national_not_pinned_to_canberra: Commonwealth entities cover the whole nation. They are rendered as a national overlay or as a floating node — not as a point in the ACT.

national.notes: Two rendering modes; UI may toggle. Floating-node mode keeps Commonwealth entities outside any state polygon.

multi_state.notes: Cross-jurisdiction entities span more than one state/territory.

non_spatial.notes: Intergovernmental bodies, ministerial councils, cabinet committees, and cross-government committees are not placed inside any single jurisdiction.

intergovernmental_rendering.rule: Render as connectors between member polities; never inside a single jurisdiction.

status_propagation.rule: Same as ux_status_rules_v41.json — spatial view inherits truth-layer flags unchanged.
